---
title: "Participant practice exercise"
description: "Practice one bounded Issue-to-pull-request contribution with GitHub Desktop."
---

Use only the assigned synthetic alias and fictional practice content. Before starting, read how to [understand and update an Issue](../start-here.qmd) and review the supported [GitHub Desktop pull-request workflow](../first-pull-request.qmd). Ask the named steward for help at any stop point described in those guides or the [steward guide](../stewards.qmd).

## Complete the practice workflow

1. Open the assigned, manually created practice Issue.
2. Identify its objective and acceptance criteria in your own words.
3. Assign yourself when repository permissions and the facilitator's instructions allow it. Otherwise, claim the Issue with a comment.
4. Post a starting-work update that states the first action and any blocker. Use the [progress-update pattern](../start-here.qmd#post-a-progress-update) rather than inventing a separate status record.
5. Create an Issue-linked branch from `main` using the branch name supplied by or agreed with the steward.
6. Open the correct repository and branch in GitHub Desktop.
7. Copy `pilot/submission-template.qmd` to `practice/submissions/<assigned-alias>.qmd`. Replace `<assigned-alias>` with the assigned lowercase alias and use only letters, numbers, and hyphens.
8. Replace only the approved synthetic fields in the copy. Do not add metadata or personal information.
9. In GitHub Desktop, inspect the complete diff for every changed file. Stop if anything unrelated or unfamiliar appears.
10. Create a focused commit, then push or publish the branch. A commit records a snapshot; pushing copies the branch to GitHub.
11. Open a pull request against `main`. Include `Fixes #<issue-number>` with the assigned practice Issue number, summarize the change, and record validation accurately.
12. Request review from the named repository steward.
13. Respond to the steward's one simulated review comment. Make and push a focused correction if the comment requests one.
14. After an authorized steward merges the pull request and the site is published, find the rendered submission under the practice area.

The detailed client steps and terminology remain in [Your first pull request](../first-pull-request.qmd); this exercise is the task sequence, not a replacement guide. If validation fails, repository access is missing, the branch is wrong, or feedback is unclear, stop and ask the steward.

## Completion states are separate

- **Participant work complete:** the participant has completed the requested edit, validation, pull request, review response, and progress record.
- **Pull-request review complete:** an authorized reviewer has accepted the proposed repository change.
- **Merged:** an authorized person has integrated the pull request into `main`.
- **Issue closed:** GitHub applies `Fixes #<issue-number>` after merge when the linked practice Issue is fully resolved. Closing is not broader acceptance.
- **Published:** the merged Quarto source has been rendered and deployed. Publication does not prove leadership or Issue acceptance.
- **Broader program complete:** determined outside this repository practice; a merged submission alone does not establish it.

Record any blocker in the authoritative practice Issue. Do not use the Quarto page, branch, commit, or pull request as a substitute for the Issue's operational record.
